title: The Silent Deterioration
description: A heart-failure patient's weight rises gradually over two weeks; each reading is within threshold, but the trend plus reported ankle swelling suggests fluid retention.

alert.notes: Gradual ~4 kg gain over 14 days; no single reading above the absolute threshold, but the upward trend triggered a trend-based alert.

gold_brief.alert_summary: Body weight has risen to 85.8 kg — roughly a 4 kg gain over two weeks. No single reading crossed the 88 kg threshold, but the steady upward trend triggered a trend-based alert. Classified Urgent.
gold_brief.patient_snapshot: 72-year-old man with HFrEF (EF 35%), hypertension, and atrial fibrillation on furosemide, carvedilol, and sacubitril-valsartan; documented dry weight 81.5 kg.

contextual_analysis.statement: The steady ~4 kg gain over 14 days is consistent with fluid retention rather than true weight gain.
This is corroborated by new patient-reported bilateral ankle swelling and two-pillow orthopnea over the same period.
The patient reports full medication adherence; recent salty takeout meals may have contributed.

risk_assessment.statement: These findings suggest early decompensated heart failure; without intervention this risks progression and hospitalization.

recommended_actions.action: Contact the patient today to assess volume status and consider a temporary furosemide (diuretic) increase per his heart-failure action plan.
Arrange near-term review with repeat weight, symptom check, and labs (renal function, electrolytes, NT-proBNP).

uncertainties_and_gaps: No labs since April 2026; current renal function and electrolytes are unknown before adjusting diuretics.
